data.title: Cardinal Energy declares CAD 0.06 dividend
data.description:
• Cardinal Energy (CJ:CA) declares CAD 0.06/share monthly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 6.04%
  • Payable Feb. 17; for shareholders of record Jan. 30; ex-div Jan. 30.
  • See CJ:CA Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: General Dynamics unit wins $988 mln U.S. Navy C5ISR modernization contract
data.description:
• General Dynamics (GDIT) a business unit of General Dynamics (GD) on Monday that its information technology unit, GDIT, was awarded a $988 million contract to modernize and integrate command, control, communications, computers, combat, intelligence, surveillance and reconnaissance (C5ISR) systems for the U.S. Navy.
  • The Ship and Air C5ISR Systems Support contract was awarded in December and includes a one-year base period, four one-year options and a six-month option.
  • Under the contract, GDIT will provide integration, engineering, procurement, logistics and installation services across all classes of surface combatant ships, as well as aircraft and shore stations, to support fleet readiness and operational effectiveness.
  • GD +0.19% after hours to $361.6565.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bill Ackman says prepayment penalties could lower mortgage rates
data.description:
Billionaire investor Bill Ackman recently proposed adding prepayment penalties to new home loans, in an unconventional way to lower mortgage rates.

Ackman said that such a move would reduce borrowing costs by about 65 basis points, according to an X post. The average 30-year fixed-rate mortgage pulled back last year, though it's still sitting above the 6% mark.

“While this feature is attractive for homeowners, it comes at a significant cost as buyers of mortgage backed securities (‘MBS’) require a significant increase in spread to compensate them for giving the borrower the option to prepay at anytime,” Ackman explained. Many U.S. mortgages are packaged into MBS.

“While the ability to prepay is a valuable option, locking in the 65 bps savings upfront over the life of the mortgage may be the difference between the borrower being able to afford the home and not being able to,” he added.

His comments come after President Donald Trump last week ordered government-sponsored enterprises Fannie Mae (FNMA) and Freddie Mac (FMCC) to acquire $200B in mortgage debt to help bring down rates. 

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Pfizer seeking priority voucher from Trump administration - report
data.description:
• Pfizer (PFE) has been in talks with the Trump administration to receive a priority voucher under a new FDA pilot program that would cut the review of one its drugs to one to two months from the standard 10 to 12 months.
  • CEO Albert Bourla told Bloomberg that the administration has spoken with the administration about the vouchers, but declined to say which drugs are under consideration for getting one.
  • Several large pharmas have already been awarded vouchers, including Eli Lilly (LLY), Novo Nordisk (NVO), and Sanofi (SNY).
  • Pfizer was one of the first drug companies to ink a deal with the Trump administration to offer discounts on some of its drugs as part of the president's "most favored nation" program.

data.title: Corn leads U.S. grain futures lower after USDA hikes crop supply estimates
data.description:
U.S. grain futures turned sharply lower Monday after the latest outlook for domestic crops showed higher ending inventories for corn and soybeans, contrary to widespread expectations for ending stocks to decline across the board.

The U.S. Department of Agriculture's latest monthly WASDE report forecast corn ending stocks at 2.23B bushels, up nearly 200M bushels from its December projection, while soybean ending stocks rose 60M bushels to 350M and wheat stocks increased by 25M bushels to 926M.

Corn led the decline in U.S. grains, due largely to record-high yields and production, along with ending stocks rising to their highest since 2018, while export sales were left unchanged at 3.2B bushels, results that disappointed many traders and analysts.

CBOT corn (C_1:COM) for March delivery closed -5.4% to $4.21 3/4 per bushel, soybeans (S_1:COM) for March delivery settled -1.1% to $10.50 1/2 per bushel, and wheat (W_1:COM) for March delivery ended -1% to $5.12 1/4 per bushel.

ETFs: (CORN), (SOYB), (WEAT), (DBA), (MOO)

"Not a single friendly number out there," Marex analyst Charlie Sernatinger said in a note after the WASDE report's release. "We have been talking about how the risk for the market was that no one was looking for larger yield in the corn, and that's what we got... This report now will dictate the fundamental tone of the market for the next 60 days, and that tone is bearish."

Meanwhile, the USDA lowered its outlook for U.S. soybean exports by 60M bushels, due to stiff competition from Brazilian crops, and raised its forecast for Brazilian soybean production to 178M metric tons, which would be a new record high.

data.title: Option Care Health reports preliminary Q4, FY 2025 results, 2026 guidance mostly above estimate
data.description:
• Option Care Health (OPCH) expects Q4 2025 net revenue of $1.46B–$1.47B, compared with an estimate of $1.46B.
  • OPCH sees GAAP diluted EPS of $0.37–$0.39, vs estimate of $0.36.
  • Q4 2025 adjusted diluted EPS is projected at $0.46–$0.49, vs estimate of $0.46, with adjusted EBITDA of $123.7M–$127.7M.
  • Full-year 2025 net revenue is expected to be $5.65B–$5.66B, compared to an estimate of $5.64B.
  • OPCH expects GAAP diluted EPS of $1.27–$1.29.
  • Full-year 2025 adjusted diluted EPS is projected at $1.72–$1.76, adjusted EBITDA of $469M–$473M, and operating cash flow below $320M.
  • For full-year 2026, the company forecasts net revenue of $5.8B–$6B, compared to estimate of $6.06B.
  • 2026 adjusted diluted EPS expected between $1.82–$1.92, vs estimate of $1.49.
  • OPCH sees 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $480M–$505M.
  • OPCH shares up 3% post-market on Monday.

data.title: Okta in spotlight as Citi adds 90-day catalyst watch
data.description:
• Okta (OKTA) was in the spotlight on Monday as Citi added a 90-day catalyst watch to the cybersecurity firm.
  • “We expect OKTA to log a strong close to FY26, marked with bookings outperformance against receded idiosyncratic headwinds, that should support upward NTM revenue revisions and expectations,” analyst Fatima Boolani wrote in a note to clients. “This is on the back of favorable read-throughs for the Identity cyber sub-segment from our CISO survey (growing prioritization among CISOs; overwhelmingly preferred avenue for ‘securing agentic AI’), and healthy cRPO upside track record over the recent few Qs, all against FY27E consensus revenue growth we deem a low hurdle (~9% YoY). Anticipating strong F4Q26 results (~3pt cRPO growth beat to LDD levels) which should fuel upward revisions to FY27E topline outlook, we expect strengthened investor conviction on NTM+ fundamentals, which against a newly launched $1B buyback position the stock for a lift against current near-trough valuation of ~4x EV/S NTM and ~15x EV/FCF NTM.”
  • Boolani has a Neutral rating and $100 price target on Okta shares.

data.title: Medtronic partnering with Precision Neuroscience on brain-computer interface
data.description:
• Medtronic (MDT) will work with Precision Neuroscience to use the latter's Layer 7 brain-computer cortical interface with the former's StealthStation surgical navigation system.
  • Layer 7 is a thin film microelectrode array that captures electrical signals from the brain to translate brain activity.
  • Precision noted that currently, neurosurgeons often use separate devices to visualize brain structure and function, impacting the speed of intraoperative decision-making. The partnership will "deliver an end-to-end system designed to provide real-time functional and structural information in the operating room," according to a news release.

data.title: Arqit Quantum files $250M mixed securities shelf offering
data.description:
• Arqit Quantum (ARQQ) filed a $250M mixed securities shelf offering on Monday.
  • Shares of the quantum computing firm were fractionally lower in extended trading after the announcement.
